brief.title: Sunday, July 12 — six things creators need to know

items.html: Apple is suing OpenAI for trade secret theft, alleging senior leaders including hardware chief Tang Tan directed a pattern of stealing confidential project codes and unreleased product details.
Meta killed its week-old Instagram AI tagging feature after backlash — the tool had let anyone generate deepfakes of public accounts without the owner's knowledge or consent.
A new ComfyUI custom node enables INT4 inference for Krea 2 Turbo on 6 GB VRAM — check your GPU tier, this opens consumer-grade runs of a previously memory-hungry model.
INT4 W4A4 quantized image models in ComfyUI now produce output nearly indistinguishable from INT8 at smaller file size — worth testing before defaulting to heavier weights.
Hugging Face's CEO warns enterprises are abandoning paid AI APIs for open-source models as costs scale — a structural shift worth tracking if your workflow depends on proprietary APIs.
A free browser-based procedural ivy and fig tree generator launched open-source, letting 3D environment artists paint and grow organic foliage on meshes in real time with no install required.

tooldrop.name: MuScriptor
tooldrop.blurb: Open-weight model from Kyutai that transcribes multi-instrument audio recordings into MIDI. Useful for musicians who want to extract notation or stems from existing tracks.

stories.title: Apple's scrapped self-driving car seeded Neural Engine AI chip architecture
stories.dek: Failed autonomous vehicle project led to development of on-device AI processing capabilities that became core to Apple Silicon's performance.
